name: macOS Defender Check
description: macOS Defender Check is a testing toolkit for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint on macOS. It validates MDE installation and health, tests Tamper Protection and Web Protection across multiple browsers, and supports custom URL indicators with CSV import. The suite produces detailed logs and CSV reports to aid pre- and post-deployment validation, troubleshooting, and compliance auditing.
author: Niklas Bruhn

securityCheck.aiSummary: The script is largely safe for an Intune/macOS Defender test but includes a high-risk tamper test that could temporarily reduce security protections. No obfuscated code, no remote code execution, no credential theft, no data exfiltration, and no hardcoded secrets detected.

noMaliciousPatterns.reason: Tamper Protection test intentionally attempts to disable Real-Time Protection via 'mdatp config real-time-protection --value disabled'. This could degrade security if misused. The script then verifies the outcome and attempts to re-enable RTP. While intended as a test, this is a sensitive operation requiring strict authorization controls, audit logging, and safe-guarded execution to avoid persistence of reduced protection.
